NTPC.q: Which city is known as the Silicon Valley of India?
NTPC.hint: It is the capital of Karnataka.
NTPC.solution: Bengaluru is known as the Silicon Valley of India.

NTPC.q: Who was the first woman PM of India?
NTPC.solution: Indira Gandhi was the first woman PM.

NTPC.q: Which city is known as the 'Heritage City' of Karnataka?
NTPC.solution: Mysore is known as the Heritage City of Karnataka.

NTPC.q: The first session of the Indian National Congress (1885) was held in:
NTPC.solution: The first session was held at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College, Bombay.

NTPC.q: Who is known as the 'Nightingale of India'?
NTPC.hint: A prominent freedom fighter and poet.
NTPC.solution: Sarojini Naidu earned this title for her lyrical poetry.

NTPC.q: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the 'Right to Education'?
NTPC.solution: Article 21A makes education a fundamental right for children aged 6-14.

NTPC.q: The 'Quit India Movement' was launched in response to the failure of:
NTPC.solution: The movement began after the failure of the Cripps Mission.

NTPC.q: Which planet is known as the 'Earth's Twin'?
NTPC.hint: Similar size and density to Earth.
NTPC.solution: Venus is called the twin due to its similar structure.

NTPC.q: The headquarters of ISRO is located in:
NTPC.solution: ISRO headquarters is in Bengaluru (Antariksh Bhavan).

NTPC.q: The concept of 'Carbon Credit' originated from which of the following?
NTPC.solution: The Kyoto Protocol introduced the carbon credit system.

ALP.q: What is the SI unit of Resistance?
ALP.hint: Represented by the Greek symbol Omega.
ALP.solution: Ohm is the standard unit of electrical resistance.

ALP.q: What is the average normal human body temperature?
Which instrument is used to measure electric current?
ALP.solution: An Ammeter measures the flow of current in Amperes.

ALP.q: The process of converting solid directly into gas is called:
ALP.hint: Think of dry ice or camphor.
ALP.solution: Sublimation is the transition without passing through the liquid phase.

ALP.q: If the length of a wire is doubled, its resistance will:
ALP.hint: Resistance is directly proportional to length.
ALP.solution: R = ρ(L/A); doubling length doubles resistance.

ALP.q: Which part of the brain controls voluntary actions and balance?
ALP.solution: The cerebellum coordinates muscle movements and balance.

ALP.q: What is the frequency of AC supply in India?
ALP.solution: India uses a 50 Hz frequency for its power grid.

ALP.q: Which mirror is used as a rear-view mirror in vehicles?
ALP.hint: It provides a wider field of view.
ALP.solution: Convex mirrors form diminished, upright images over a large area.

ALP.q: Sound waves in air are always:
ALP.hint: Particles move parallel to the wave.
ALP.solution: Sound waves are longitudinal mechanical waves.

ALP.q: The escape velocity from Earth's surface is approximately:
ALP.solution: It is roughly 11.2 km per second.

JE.q: Which material has the highest electrical conductivity?
JE.hint: It's a noble metal, more conductive than copper.
JE.solution: Silver has the highest conductivity among all metals.

JE.q: What is the standard formula for Ohm's Law?
JE.solution: V=IR is the fundamental law of electricity.

JE.q: Pascal is the SI unit for:
JE.solution: Pascal (Pa) is the unit used to measure pressure.

JE.hint: To ensure no current is drawn from the circuit.
JE.solution: Infinite resistance prevents interference with the measured circuit.

JE.q: Which alloy is used in the heating element of an electric iron?
JE.solution: Nichrome is used due to its high resistivity and melting point.

JE.q: The modulus of elasticity for a perfectly rigid body is:
JE.solution: Since deformation is zero, the modulus (Stress/Strain) is infinite.

JE.q: Bernoulli’s principle is based on the law of conservation of:
JE.solution: It is a statement of the conservation of energy for flowing fluids.

JE.q: In a 4-stroke engine, the strokes occur in which order?
JE.solution: The standard cycle is Intake → Compression → Power → Exhaust.

JE.q: Which logic gate is known as the 'Universal Gate'?
JE.hint: Can realize any other logic gate.
JE.solution: NAND and NOR are universal gates.

JE.q: The coefficient of friction depends on:
JE.solution: It depends solely on the material and nature of the contacting surfaces.

GroupD.q: What is the chemical symbol for Iron?
GroupD.solution: Fe is the symbol for Iron.

GroupD.q: Which organelle is the 'Power House' of the cell?
GroupD.solution: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's supply of ATP.

GroupD.q: Which vitamin is synthesized in our body in the presence of sunlight?
GroupD.solution: Ultraviolet rays trigger the synthesis of Vitamin D in the skin.

GroupD.q: Who is known as the 'Father of the Indian Constitution'?
GroupD.solution: Dr. Ambedkar played the lead role in drafting the Constitution.

GroupD.q: Which river is often called the 'Sorrow of Bihar' due to frequent floods?
GroupD.solution: The Kosi river causes massive annual floods in Bihar.

GroupD.q: The Planning Commission was replaced by which body in 2015?
GroupD.solution: NITI Aayog acts as the premier policy 'Think Tank' of India.

GroupD.q: Which of these is a Kharif crop?
GroupD.solution: Rice is a major Kharif crop, unlike Wheat which is Rabi.

GroupD.q: What is the pH value of pure water?
GroupD.solution: Pure water has a neutral pH of 7.

GroupD.q: Who won the first Nobel Prize for India?
Which gas is used in fire extinguishers?
GroupD.hint: It displaces oxygen to put out the fire.
GroupD.solution: CO2 is effective because it is non-combustible and heavier than air.
